name: Bbqed Chile-Marinated Pork Spareribs
recipeIngredient: 8 dried New Mexican red chiles, seeded
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine the pork spareribs and water to cover. Bring to a boil and simmer the ribs, skimming any froth as necessary, for about 50 minutes. Drain the ribs well and pat them dry.
2. While the ribs are simmering, puree the chiles, water, ketchup, garlic, vinegar, brown sugar, salt, tequila, vegetable oil, cumin, and allspice in a blender until smooth. Reserve some of the chili sauce for coating the ribs.
3. Coat the ribs generously with the chili sauce, reserving the remaining sauce in a small bowl covered with plastic wrap for at least 8 hours or overnight.
4. Let the ribs stand at room temperature for 1 hour before grilling them on an oiled rack set 5-6 inches over the heat source for 6 minutes on each side.
5. Simmer the reserved chili sauce in a small saucepan for 3 minutes and serve with the ribs.
